expectations.text: Response mentions at least 2 of the 5 hero formulas
expectations.evidence: MISS: Doesn't name the canonical hero family at all. Invents its own option labels (A Status quo, B Static Ken Burns, C Shutter-Wipe, D Crossfade-Slideshow, E Split-Hero). 'Shutter-Wipe' (option C) is NOT a canonical hero pattern — it is a 6th, invented pattern. This is exactly what SKILL.md posture rule #4 forbids: 'Pick from this list — don't invent a sixth.'

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend lens shader hero
expectations.evidence: Not recommended (doesn't surface as an option at all).

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend filmstrip wipe hero
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL: Recommends 'Shutter-Wipe' (clip-path:inset() top→bottom). This is mechanically very close to motorrad's filmstrip wipe (also a clip-path inset animation), just oriented vertically. The agent doesn't know it's reinventing the motorrad signature in a new direction.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend replacing with static + plain gradient
expectations.evidence: Option B (static Ken Burns) is mentioned but not recommended.

Cites brand-spec phrase 'slow focus shifts, soft light sweeps, crop marks, aperture/glass/film-grain details' and mentions 'hero-pd', 'hero-pt', Aperture-Overlay, Lens-Streaks. Plausible-sounding but misidentifies which hero pattern belongs to portrait — recommends removing the iris and replacing with an invented variation. The justification is performance-led, not identity-led.

expectations.text: Code skeleton uses var(--ease), clamp(), dark section markers
expectations.evidence: Uses var(--font-display), var(--font-mono), var(--accent), clamp(). Missing var(--ease) — uses cubic-bezier(.22,.9,.22,1) inline. Has data-header-theme="dark". Mostly OK.

notes: The critical failure: recommends a hero pattern that doesn't exist in the canonical 5 (a vertical shutter-wipe) and tells the user to remove the iris reveal — which is the portrait section's signature. The code is technically competent and the performance argument is solid, but the recommendation breaks the design-system invariant that each section has its own hero formula. This is precisely the 'flatten the metaphor' failure SKILL.md warns about.
